summary: Flemish Renaissance painter of peasant genre scenes. Known for Landscape with the Fall of Icarus (ploughman ignores drowning Icarus), Hunters in the Snow, The Peasant Wedding, Netherlandish Proverbs, The Tower of Babel, Mad Meg, The Triumph of Death. Father of 'Velvet' and 'Hell' Bruegel.

works.name: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus (c. 1560)
works.description: Ploughman in foreground ignores title figure's legs sticking out of sea; ship sails past; shepherd stares skyward. Existing version may be a copy. Inspired Auden's 'Musee des Beaux Arts.' Wikipedia

images.caption: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus

clues.clue: Ploughman ignores title figure's legs sticking out of the sea; ship sails past; shepherd on cliff stares skyward; existing version may be a copy
examples: In a landscape by this painter, the only visible part of the title figure is a pair of legs sticking out of the sea
a ship sails past the flailing feet of the title drowning youth

clues.clue: Inspired W.H. Auden's poem 'Musee des Beaux Arts' ('About suffering they were never wrong')
examples: The speaker of W.H. Auden's poem 'Musee des Beaux Arts' mentions the splash made by Icarus

clues.clue: Also inspired Wislawa Szymborska poem about Two Monkeys
examples: Szymborska takes an 'exam on the history of mankind' in a poem about a painting by this artist

works.name: Hunters in the Snow (1565)
works.description: Three hunters and dogs trudge through wintry landscape; skaters on frozen lake in distance; dead fox carried. Part of Labors of the Months series. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Three hunters and dogs trudge through winter landscape; skaters on frozen lake in distance; one hunter carries dead fox; tavern sign with St. Hubertus and deer
examples: three men and their dogs return from an unsuccessful trip, and silhouettes can be seen ice skating in the distance
In a painting by this artist, a man carrying a dead fox and his companion walk on a hill in the foreground

clues.clue: Part of Labors of the Months series (six paintings; five extant: The Gloomy Day, Haymaking, The Harvesters, The Return of the Herd)
examples: This artist's paintings Haymaking, The Gloomy Day, and The Return of the Herd are among the five extant works in his series of six landscapes

clues.clue: Used by Abbas Kiarostami in first shot of posthumous film 24 Frames
examples: Birds and smoke from a painting by this artist begin to move in the first shot of Abbas Kiarostami's posthumous film 24 Frames

works.name: The Peasant Wedding
works.description: Celebratory feast in barn; bowls of soup/porridge on makeshift tray made from a door; boy in red cap licks plate; paper crown in background.

clues.clue: Feast in barn; two men carry soup/porridge on a door used as tray; boy in red cap licks plate; paper crown on green cloth
examples: Bowls of soup and porridge rest on a makeshift tray made of a door
a boy with a red cap covering his eyes licks a plate

works.name: Netherlandish Proverbs (1559)
works.description: Dense painting illustrating 100+ Dutch proverbs/idioms. Also called The Blue Cloak. Includes biting a column, tarts on a roof, big fish eating little fish, man belling a cat.

clues.clue: Dense painting illustrating 100+ Dutch proverbs including biting the column, tarts on roof, man belling a cat; also called The Blue Cloak
examples: A roof is tiled with tarts in a dense, zany painting by this artist that illustrates idioms like 'biting the column'
a man in a suit of armor places a bell on a cat

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Father of painters nicknamed 'Hell' (Pieter Brueghel the Younger) and 'Velvet' (Jan Brueghel the Elder). Other works: The Tower of Babel (Colosseum-based, Nimrod), Mad Meg/Dull Gret (woman leading army to pillage Hell), The Triumph of Death (skeletons), The Fall of the Rebel Angels, The Blind Leading the Blind, Children's Games, The Misanthrope, The Fight Between Carnival and Lent, The Census at Bethlehem, The Preaching of St. John the Baptist, Two Monkeys, Winter Landscape with Bird Trap, Massacre of the Innocents. Made Large Landscapes print series with Hieronymus Cock. 'World landscape' style from Patinir.

examples: This father of painters nicknamed 'Hell' and 'Velvet'

clues.clue: The Tower of Babel: may have used Colosseum engravings; King Nimrod stands before unfinished structure
examples: King Nimrod standing before a massive unfinished structure in one of his three versions of The Tower of Babel

clues.clue: Mad Meg/Dull Gret: sword-wielding woman leads army of women into mouth of Hell; frying pan in basket
examples: The title sword-wielding figure leads an army of women into the mouth of Hell

clues.clue: The Triumph of Death: skeletons in togas playing trumpets, executing humans
examples: sword-wielding, trumpet-playing skeletons who vary from their skeleton brethren who execute humans

clues.clue: The Fall of the Rebel Angels: rebels transforming into fish, butterflies, bizarre creatures; Bosch-influenced; Michael in golden armor
examples: The title characters are transforming into fish, butterflies, and bizarre hybrid-animal-things

clues.clue: The Blind Leading the Blind: six men stumbling diagonally, from Matthew 15:14
examples: six men stumbling diagonally across the canvas

clues.clue: Large Landscapes print series with publisher Hieronymus Cock
examples: he made the Large Landscapes print series with publisher Hieronymus Cock

clues.clue: Two Monkeys chained under archway overlooking Antwerp
examples: Two of these animals chained together can be seen under an archway overlooking the city of Antwerp

clues.clue: Michael Frayn's novel Headlong: character thinks he discovered a painting from The Months
examples: In Frayn's novel Headlong, Martin Clay thinks he has discovered a painting in this artist's series The Months

clues.clue: The Harvesters: family feasting next to grain stacks; peasants relaxing under tree
examples: its focus is a ripe wheat field and some peasants relaxing and eating under a tree

comprehensive_summary:
Pieter Bruegel the Elder was a Flemish Renaissance painter renowned for peasant genre scenes and landscapes. He is most commonly identified by Landscape with the Fall of Icarus and Hunters in the Snow.

Landscape with the Fall of Icarus shows a ploughman ignoring Icarus's legs sticking out of the sea as a ship sails past. The existing version may be a copy. It inspired W.H. Auden's 'Musee des Beaux Arts' and Wislawa Szymborska's poetry.

Hunters in the Snow is part of his Labors of the Months series (five of six survive). Three hunters with dogs trudge through a winter landscape with skaters on a frozen lake. One carries a dead fox. Other surviving panels include The Gloomy Day, Haymaking, The Harvesters, and The Return of the Herd.

The Peasant Wedding shows a feast in a barn with soup carried on a door, a boy in a red cap, and a paper crown. Netherlandish Proverbs (The Blue Cloak) illustrates 100+ Dutch idioms.

Other major works include The Tower of Babel (Nimrod, Colosseum-based), Mad Meg (woman leading army to Hell), The Triumph of Death (skeletons), The Fall of the Rebel Angels (Bosch-influenced), The Blind Leading the Blind, Children's Games, The Misanthrope, The Fight Between Carnival and Lent, and Two Monkeys. He collaborated with publisher Hieronymus Cock and was the father of painters 'Velvet' (Jan) and 'Hell' (Pieter the Younger).
